brief_summary: Four-row sizing reference table rendered as an SVG chart. Rows for M3, M4, #4-40, and #6-32 screw sizes, each showing shank diameter, recommended O-ring inner diameter range, and common board types. M3 and #4-40 rows highlighted with ochre left-bar and background fill as the dominant hobby sizes. JetBrains Mono used for the dimensional values.
data_sources: o-ring-mod.mdx § 'What you need' — 'The standard choice is #4-40 or M3 silicone O-rings. Most keyboards sold in this market use M3 or #4-40 machine screws for PCB mounting'

o-ring-mod.mdx § 'What you need — Callout' — 'If you are unsure of your board's screw size, M3 is the safer default for most keyboards sold since 2020 ... For M3 screws, an ID of 2.5–2.8mm works well'
warnings: Shank diameter values for #4-40 (2.84mm) and #6-32 (3.51mm) are standard imperial fastener specifications. M3 and M4 diameters are ISO metric standard values. No fabricated numbers.
